notes: No tweaks needed, runs perfectly out of the box! I didn't even remember there wasn't a native build! The only thing I did not test was VR. Other than that, everything seems fine!
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Works great out of the box. Performance is good, 60 fps at ultra.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Installs and runs without any problems. Performance is superb too.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Seems to run perfectly, even with lots of other stuff running. Had no issues detecting my resolution etc, all objects render beautifully (I had one visual glitch the entire time playing, which is about 90 minutes so far). Feels native. Screenshots folder even opens when I close the game (if I've taken screenshots) so I can get them out of the Proton/Wine system easily. Very impressive for a game not built for Linux.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Runs flawlessly. Game handles resizing the window well.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Seems to suffer some random freezes when loading terrain.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Game runs with no performance issues, but won't render the surface of the planets. Everything appears as a clean sphere.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Works fine when using the 3.16 branch.  However when using the newer 4.2 branch the lighting is broken. Also high res packs will crash the game.  So I recommend using an older branch of proton.  It is more stable for this game.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Runs game just as well as windows version
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Game runs without any performance or stability issues. Graphics are often broken. Nebula do not render and planets appear as smooth, solid colored spheres.
title: SpaceEngine

notes:
Can have perfomance hits at times, and crashes semi-frequently.
Otherwise nothing else preventing the game from being played.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Very smooth. No problem at all.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Runs out of the box just as well as the windows version, which itself has some hitching at times (this is an early access game still). Seen no underlining issues that differ.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Had troubles previously with older Mesa versions and the last free version of Space Engine (no terrain, planets just spheres -> had to force OpenGL 3.3 using Mesa's environment variables), but with current Space Engine, Steam Play and Mesa 19.0.8 it's running quite well out of the box. There are occasional graphics glitches with terrain, especially after changing graphics settings, but I would expect those to be caused by the game (which is still early access) and not related to Steam Play or Mesa.
title: SpaceEngine

notes: Runs as good as the Windows version
title: SpaceEngine

notes:
The loading banner appeared, and a window popped up asking if I want to disable shading on planets.  If I clicked yes, it would load all the way through, and go to the main menu, which was just a white screen where I couldn't do anything.

If I clicked no on the box, the game would close.

If I ignored the box, it would disappear, and the game would be stuck loading.
title: SpaceEngine
